response.justification: Bogdan is a strong generalist software engineer with solid TypeScript and distributed systems experience, currently at Stripe working on AI-agent infrastructure and MCP code generators — directly relevant to the role's key technologies. However, the resume does not demonstrate the required 8+ years of AI-specific development; AI work appears limited to the most recent role (April 2025 to present). Critical stack gaps are evident: Python is absent from the skills section, and there is no mention of RAG architectures, vector databases, LLM evaluation pipelines, or multi-agent frameworks such as LangGraph or CrewAI. The customer-facing consulting dimension of this Staff Forward Deployed role is also unaddressed. A screening call to probe the depth of AI work at Stripe and any undocumented Python or GenAI experience would clarify whether these are real gaps or simply resume omissions.
strengths: Currently contributing to AI-agent infrastructure and MCP server tooling at Stripe, directly aligning with a key technology listed in the job description
Demonstrated Staff-level seniority at multiple companies with evidence of technical leadership, mentoring, and knowledge-sharing (workshops, talks, open-source projects)
Deep TypeScript and cloud infrastructure experience (AWS CDK at Shopify, Node.js at scale at Dunnhumby), satisfying one of the two core language requirements
concerns: Python is entirely absent from the resume and skills list, despite being a primary required language for the role
No mention of RAG architectures, vector databases, LLM optimization, or multi-agent frameworks — the core GenAI technical stack is unrepresented beyond MCP tooling
AI-specific production experience appears to begin in April 2025, falling far short of the 8-year minimum; prior roles are traditional web/backend/data-integration engineering with no AI framing

key_factors.factor: MCP and agentic tooling at Stripe
Missing Python and RAG/vector DB skills
